question: SIG-39: Are meta-historian, meta-tooler, and meta-experimenter balanced in dispatch, and can role-aware recommendations improve meta utilization?
expect: Historian >50% of meta lanes (dominant maintenance role). Tooler <20%. dispatch_optimizer.py recommends specific meta-role after change.
method: Classified 117 DOMEX-META lanes (MERGED/ABANDONED/ACTIVE from SWARM-LANES + archive) using keyword-based role classifier. 3 core roles: historian (repair, stale, retest), tooler (build, wire, enforce, module), experimenter (measure, test, audit, falsify). Role assigned by highest keyword match count; ties → mixed. Added _classify_meta_role() and _get_meta_role_stats() to dispatch_optimizer.py. Meta-role advisory printed when meta appears in UCB1 output.

implementation.output_changes: UCB1 mode prints meta-role distribution and suggested role when meta is in display
implementation.json_changes: meta domain result dict includes 'meta_roles' key with role stats

actual: Experimenter dominates at 34.2%, NOT historian (18.8%). Tooler most underserved (13.7%). Measurement bias (L-895, L-910) applies within meta too: meta defaults to measuring, not building or maintaining. dispatch_optimizer.py now shows meta-role advisory.
diff: Expected historian >50% — WRONG (18.8%). Expected tooler <20% — CONFIRMED (13.7%). The L-895 measurement gravity applies at sub-domain level: meta-experimenter crowds out meta-tooler and meta-historian at 2.5:1 and 1.8:1 ratios respectively. Unclassified 21.4% suggests intents are too vague for keyword classification — structural enforcement of role field in open_lane.py would be more reliable.
